```{python}
#| label: setup-analises-avancadas
# Setup
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns
from datetime import datetime
import warnings
from matplotlib import font_manager as fm

warnings.filterwarnings('ignore')

# Configurações
sns.set_style("whitegrid")
plt.rcParams['figure.figsize'] = (14, 8)

# Cores
COLORS = {
    'P1': '#3498db',
    'P2': '#e74c3c',
    'neutral': '#95a5a6'
}

# Carrega dados
df = pd.read_csv('final_dataframe_enriched_complete.csv')
df['timestamp'] = pd.to_datetime(df['timestamp'])

print("✅ Setup completo!")
print(f"📊 Dataset: {len(df):,} mensagens")
```


## 📊 Código: Análise Completa de Viagens

```{python}
#| label: analise-viagens-completa
#| fig-cap: "Análise Detalhada: Impacto das Viagens"

import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np
from datetime import datetime, timedelta

# Define cores
COLORS = {
    'P1': '#3498db',
    'P2': '#e74c3c', 
    'neutral': '#95a5a6',
    'juntos': '#2ecc71'
}

# ============================================================================
# DATAS PRECISAS DAS VIAGENS
# ============================================================================

viagens = [
    {
        'nome': 'Chapada/BSB (1ª viagem)',
        'inicio': '2024-12-12',
        'fim': '2024-12-24',
        'tipo': 'Marlon @ BSB',
        'marco_especial': '2024-12-16',  # 1ª vez juntos
        'marco_nome': '💕 1ª Vez Juntos'
    },
    {
        'nome': 'BSB (Janeiro)',
        'inicio': '2025-01-23',
        'fim': '2025-01-29',  # ← CORRIGIDO (6 dias)
        'tipo': 'Marlon @ BSB',
        'marco_especial': None,
        'marco_nome': None
    },
    {
        'nome': 'Letícia SP (Hostel)',
        'inicio': '2025-02-06',
        'fim': '2025-02-10',
        'tipo': 'Letícia @ SP',
        'marco_especial': None,
        'marco_nome': None
    },
    {
        'nome': 'BSB (Carnaval)',
        'inicio': '2025-02-27',
        'fim': '2025-03-10',
        'tipo': 'Marlon @ BSB',
        'marco_especial': None,
        'marco_nome': '🦷 Arrancou dente'
    },
    {
        'nome': 'BSB (Abril-Maio)',
        'inicio': '2025-04-16',
        'fim': '2025-05-05',
        'tipo': 'Marlon @ BSB',
        'marco_especial': None,
        'marco_nome': None
    },
    {
        'nome': 'Letícia Sorocaba',
        'inicio': '2025-05-17',
        'fim': '2025-05-24',
        'tipo': 'Letícia @ Sorocaba',
        'marco_especial': None,
        'marco_nome': None
    },
    {
        'nome': 'Letícia SP (Trampo)',
        'inicio': '2025-07-21',
        'fim': '2025-07-26',
        'tipo': 'Letícia @ SP',
        'marco_especial': None,
        'marco_nome': None
    },
    {
        'nome': 'BSB (2 meses!)',
        'inicio': '2025-08-22',
        'fim': '2025-10-11',
        'tipo': 'Marlon @ BSB',
        'marco_especial': None,
        'marco_nome': '🏠 2 meses juntos'
    },
    {
        'nome': 'BSB (Niver Letícia)',
        'inicio': '2025-11-05',
        'fim': '2025-11-19',
        'tipo': 'Marlon @ BSB',
        'marco_especial': '2025-11-04',
        'marco_nome': '🎂 Aniversário Letícia'
    },
    {
        'nome': 'BSB (Fim de ano!)',
        'inicio': '2025-12-12',
        'fim': '2026-01-04',
        'tipo': 'Marlon @ BSB',
        'marco_especial': None,
        'marco_nome': None
    }
]

# Converte para DataFrame
viagens_df = pd.DataFrame(viagens)
viagens_df['inicio'] = pd.to_datetime(viagens_df['inicio'])
viagens_df['fim'] = pd.to_datetime(viagens_df['fim'])
viagens_df['duracao_dias'] = (viagens_df['fim'] - viagens_df['inicio']).dt.days + 1  # +1 para incluir último dia

print("="*80)
print("📅 VIAGENS REGISTRADAS")
print("="*80)
print(f"\nTotal de viagens: {len(viagens_df)}\n")
print(viagens_df[['nome', 'inicio', 'fim', 'duracao_dias', 'tipo']].to_string(index=False))

# Estatísticas
total_dias_juntos = viagens_df['duracao_dias'].sum()
total_dias_ano = 366  # Out/2024 a Out/2025
pct_juntos = (total_dias_juntos / total_dias_ano) * 100

print(f"\n📊 Resumo:")
print(f"   • Total de dias juntos: {total_dias_juntos} dias")
print(f"   • Percentual do ano: {pct_juntos:.1f}%")
print(f"   • Viagem mais longa: {viagens_df['duracao_dias'].max()} dias ({viagens_df.loc[viagens_df['duracao_dias'].idxmax(), 'nome']})")
print(f"   • Viagem mais curta: {viagens_df['duracao_dias'].min()} dias ({viagens_df.loc[viagens_df['duracao_dias'].idxmin(), 'nome']})")

print("\n" + "="*80)
```


```{python}
#| label: load-emoji-helper

"""
Emoji Helper para Matplotlib
Usa emojis como imagens (sempre funciona!)
"""

from matplotlib.offsetbox import OffsetImage, AnnotationBbox
from PIL import Image
import requests
from io import BytesIO
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

class MultiEmojiHelper:
    """Helper para adicionar emojis coloridos em gráficos matplotlib."""
    
    # Mapeamento emoji → código unicode
    EMOJI_CODES = {
        '😀': '1f600', '😂': '1f602', '😍': '1f60d', '😢': '1f622',
        '😭': '1f62d', '😡': '1f621', '🤔': '1f914', '🤣': '1f923',
        '😊': '1f60a', '😎': '1f60e', '🥰': '1f970', '😘': '1f618',
        '🤗': '1f917', '😳': '1f633', '🥺': '1f97a', '😏': '1f60f',
        '😌': '1f60c', '😔': '1f614', '😪': '1f62a', '🤤': '1f924',
        '❤️': '2764-fe0f', '💔': '1f494', '💕': '1f495', '💯': '1f4af',
        '🔥': '1f525', '✨': '2728', '⭐': '2b50', '💫': '1f4ab',
        '👍': '1f44d', '👎': '1f44e', '👏': '1f44f', '🙏': '1f64f',
        '🤝': '1f91d', '💪': '1f4aa', '✌️': '270c-fe0f', '🤞': '1f91e',
        '🤙': '1f919', '👌': '1f44c', '🤘': '1f918', '🖤': '1f5a4',
        '💙': '1f499', '💚': '1f49a', '💛': '1f49b', '🧡': '1f9e1',
        '💜': '1f49c', '🤍': '1f90d', '🤎': '1f90e', '💖': '1f496',
        '💗': '1f497', '💘': '1f498', '💝': '1f49d', '💞': '1f49e',
        '📊': '1f4ca', '📈': '1f4c8', '📉': '1f4c9', '💰': '1f4b0',
        '📅': '1f4c5', '📱': '1f4f1', '💻': '1f4bb', '🎨': '1f3a8',
        '🚀': '1f680', '🎉': '1f389', '🎁': '1f381', '🏆': '1f3c6',
        '✅': '2705', '❌': '274c', '⚠️': '26a0-fe0f', '💬': '1f4ac',
        '🙈': '1f648', '🙉': '1f649', '🙊': '1f64a', '🐵': '1f435',
        '🌹': '1f339', '🌸': '1f338', '🌺': '1f33a', '🌻': '1f33b',
    }
    
    # Templates de URL por biblioteca
    LIBRARIES = {
        'twemoji': {
            'url_template': 'https://em-content.zobj.net/thumbs/120/twitter/348/{name}_{code}.png',
            'name_map': {
                '😀': 'grinning-face', '😂': 'face-with-tears-of-joy',
                '😍': 'smiling-face-with-heart-eyes', '😢': 'crying-face',
                '😭': 'loudly-crying-face', '😡': 'pouting-face',
                '🤔': 'thinking-face', '🤣': 'rolling-on-the-floor-laughing',
                '😊': 'smiling-face-with-smiling-eyes', '😎': 'smiling-face-with-sunglasses',
                '🥰': 'smiling-face-with-hearts', '😘': 'face-blowing-a-kiss',
                '🤗': 'hugging-face', '😳': 'flushed-face', '🥺': 'pleading-face',
                '😏': 'smirking-face', '😌': 'relieved-face', '😔': 'pensive-face',
                '😪': 'sleepy-face', '🤤': 'drooling-face',
                '❤️': 'red-heart', '💔': 'broken-heart', '💕': 'two-hearts',
                '💯': 'hundred-points', '🔥': 'fire', '✨': 'sparkles',
                '⭐': 'star', '💫': 'dizzy', '👍': 'thumbs-up', '👎': 'thumbs-down',
                '👏': 'clapping-hands', '🙏': 'folded-hands', '🤝': 'handshake',
                '💪': 'flexed-biceps', '🤙': 'call-me-hand', '👌': 'ok-hand',
                '🤘': 'sign-of-the-horns', '🖤': 'black-heart', '💙': 'blue-heart',
                '💚': 'green-heart', '💛': 'yellow-heart', '🧡': 'orange-heart',
                '💜': 'purple-heart', '🤍': 'white-heart', '🤎': 'brown-heart',
                '💖': 'sparkling-heart', '💗': 'growing-heart', '💘': 'heart-with-arrow',
                '💝': 'heart-with-ribbon', '💞': 'revolving-hearts',
                '📊': 'bar-chart', '📈': 'chart-increasing', '📉': 'chart-decreasing',
                '💰': 'money-bag', '📅': 'calendar', '📱': 'mobile-phone',
                '💻': 'laptop', '🎨': 'artist-palette', '🚀': 'rocket',
                '🎉': 'party-popper', '🎁': 'wrapped-gift', '🏆': 'trophy',
                '✅': 'check-mark-button', '❌': 'cross-mark',
                '🙈': 'see-no-evil-monkey', '🙉': 'hear-no-evil-monkey',
                '🙊': 'speak-no-evil-monkey', '🐵': 'monkey-face',
                '🌹': 'rose', '🌸': 'cherry-blossom', '🌺': 'hibiscus',
                '🌻': 'sunflower',
            }
        },
    }
    
    def __init__(self, library='twemoji'):
        """Inicializa o helper."""
        self.library = library
        self._cache = {}
        print(f"✅ MultiEmojiHelper inicializado com biblioteca: {library}")
    
    def _get_emoji_url(self, emoji):
        """Gera URL do emoji baseado na biblioteca escolhida."""
        if emoji not in self.EMOJI_CODES:
            return None
        
        code = self.EMOJI_CODES[emoji]
        lib_config = self.LIBRARIES[self.library]
        url_template = lib_config['url_template']
        
        # Para twemoji que usa nomes
        if 'name_map' in lib_config:
            if emoji in lib_config['name_map']:
                name = lib_config['name_map'][emoji]
                return url_template.format(name=name, code=code)
            else:
                # Fallback
                return f"https://em-content.zobj.net/thumbs/120/twitter/348/{code}.png"
        
        return None
    
    def _get_emoji_image(self, emoji):
        """Baixa e cacheia a imagem do emoji."""
        cache_key = f"{self.library}_{emoji}"
        
        if cache_key in self._cache:
            return self._cache[cache_key]
        
        url = self._get_emoji_url(emoji)
        if url is None:
            print(f"⚠️  Emoji '{emoji}' não disponível")
            return None
        
        try:
            response = requests.get(url, timeout=5)
            if response.status_code != 200:
                print(f"❌ Erro {response.status_code} ao baixar: {url}")
                return None
            
            img = Image.open(BytesIO(response.content))
            self._cache[cache_key] = img
            return img
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"❌ Erro ao baixar emoji '{emoji}': {e}")
            return None
    
    def add_emoji(self, ax, emoji, x, y, zoom=0.1, xycoords='data'):
        """Adiciona emoji no gráfico."""
        img = self._get_emoji_image(emoji)
        if img is None:
            return None
        
        imagebox = OffsetImage(img, zoom=zoom)
        ab = AnnotationBbox(imagebox, (x, y), 
                           frameon=False, 
                           xycoords=xycoords,
                           box_alignment=(0.5, 0.5))
        ax.add_artist(ab)
        return ab

# Cria instância global
emoji = MultiEmojiHelper('twemoji')

print("\n📖 Helper carregado! Use: emoji.add_emoji(ax, '😀', x, y)")
```



```{python}
#| label: analise-emojis-coloridos
#| fig-cap: "Análise Completa de Emojis (coloridos!)"
#| fig-height: 14

# ... (todo o código da função analyze_emojis_colorful que te passei)

# Executa análise
emoji_all, emoji_p1, emoji_p2 = analyze_emojis_colorful(df, emoji)
```


```{python}
#| label: emoji-analysis-complete

# 1. Carrega helper
from matplotlib.offsetbox import OffsetImage, AnnotationBbox
# ... (código do MultiEmojiHelper)

emoji = MultiEmojiHelper('twemoji')

# 2. Define função de análise
def analyze_emojis_colorful(df, emoji_helper):
    # ... (código completo)
    pass

# 3. Executa
emoji_all, emoji_p1, emoji_p2 = analyze_emojis_colorful(df, emoji)
```


```{python}
#| label: emoji-analysis-complete
#| fig-cap: "Análise Completa de Emojis (coloridos!)"
#| fig-height: 14

# ============================================================================
# 1. CARREGA EMOJI HELPER
# ============================================================================

from matplotlib.offsetbox import OffsetImage, AnnotationBbox
from PIL import Image
import requests
from io import BytesIO
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

class MultiEmojiHelper:
    """Helper para adicionar emojis coloridos em gráficos matplotlib."""
    
    EMOJI_CODES = {
        '😀': '1f600', '😂': '1f602', '😍': '1f60d', '😢': '1f622',
        '😭': '1f62d', '😡': '1f621', '🤔': '1f914', '🤣': '1f923',
        '😊': '1f60a', '😎': '1f60e', '🥰': '1f970', '😘': '1f618',
        '🤗': '1f917', '😳': '1f633', '🥺': '1f97a', '😏': '1f60f',
        '😌': '1f60c', '😔': '1f614', '😪': '1f62a', '🤤': '1f924',
        '❤️': '2764-fe0f', '💔': '1f494', '💕': '1f495', '💯': '1f4af',
        '🔥': '1f525', '✨': '2728', '⭐': '2b50', '💫': '1f4ab',
        '👍': '1f44d', '👎': '1f44e', '👏': '1f44f', '🙏': '1f64f',
        '🤝': '1f91d', '💪': '1f4aa', '✌️': '270c-fe0f', '🤞': '1f91e',
        '🤙': '1f919', '👌': '1f44c', '🤘': '1f918', '🖤': '1f5a4',
        '💙': '1f499', '💚': '1f49a', '💛': '1f49b', '🧡': '1f9e1',
        '💜': '1f49c', '🤍': '1f90d', '🤎': '1f90e', '💖': '1f496',
        '💗': '1f497', '💘': '1f498', '💝': '1f49d', '💞': '1f49e',
        '📊': '1f4ca', '📈': '1f4c8', '📉': '1f4c9', '💰': '1f4b0',
        '📅': '1f4c5', '📱': '1f4f1', '💻': '1f4bb', '🎨': '1f3a8',
        '🚀': '1f680', '🎉': '1f389', '🎁': '1f381', '🏆': '1f3c6',
        '✅': '2705', '❌': '274c', '⚠️': '26a0-fe0f', '💬': '1f4ac',
        '🙈': '1f648', '🙉': '1f649', '🙊': '1f64a', '🐵': '1f435',
        '🌹': '1f339', '🌸': '1f338', '🌺': '1f33a', '🌻': '1f33b',
    }
    
    LIBRARIES = {
        'twemoji': {
            'url_template': 'https://em-content.zobj.net/thumbs/120/twitter/348/{name}_{code}.png',
            'name_map': {
                '😀': 'grinning-face', '😂': 'face-with-tears-of-joy',
                '😍': 'smiling-face-with-heart-eyes', '😢': 'crying-face',
                '😭': 'loudly-crying-face', '😡': 'pouting-face',
                '🤔': 'thinking-face', '🤣': 'rolling-on-the-floor-laughing',
                '😊': 'smiling-face-with-smiling-eyes', '😎': 'smiling-face-with-sunglasses',
                '🥰': 'smiling-face-with-hearts', '😘': 'face-blowing-a-kiss',
                '🤗': 'hugging-face', '😳': 'flushed-face', '🥺': 'pleading-face',
                '😏': 'smirking-face', '😌': 'relieved-face', '😔': 'pensive-face',
                '😪': 'sleepy-face', '🤤': 'drooling-face',
                '❤️': 'red-heart', '💔': 'broken-heart', '💕': 'two-hearts',
                '💯': 'hundred-points', '🔥': 'fire', '✨': 'sparkles',
                '⭐': 'star', '💫': 'dizzy', '👍': 'thumbs-up', '👎': 'thumbs-down',
                '👏': 'clapping-hands', '🙏': 'folded-hands', '🤝': 'handshake',
                '💪': 'flexed-biceps', '🤙': 'call-me-hand', '👌': 'ok-hand',
                '🤘': 'sign-of-the-horns', '🖤': 'black-heart', '💙': 'blue-heart',
                '💚': 'green-heart', '💛': 'yellow-heart', '🧡': 'orange-heart',
                '💜': 'purple-heart', '🤍': 'white-heart', '🤎': 'brown-heart',
                '💖': 'sparkling-heart', '💗': 'growing-heart', '💘': 'heart-with-arrow',
                '💝': 'heart-with-ribbon', '💞': 'revolving-hearts',
                '📊': 'bar-chart', '📈': 'chart-increasing', '📉': 'chart-decreasing',
                '💰': 'money-bag', '📅': 'calendar', '📱': 'mobile-phone',
                '💻': 'laptop', '🎨': 'artist-palette', '🚀': 'rocket',
                '🎉': 'party-popper', '🎁': 'wrapped-gift', '🏆': 'trophy',
                '✅': 'check-mark-button', '❌': 'cross-mark',
                '🙈': 'see-no-evil-monkey', '🙉': 'hear-no-evil-monkey',
                '🙊': 'speak-no-evil-monkey', '🐵': 'monkey-face',
                '🌹': 'rose', '🌸': 'cherry-blossom', '🌺': 'hibiscus',
                '🌻': 'sunflower',
            }
        },
    }
    
    def __init__(self, library='twemoji'):
        self.library = library
        self._cache = {}
    
    def _get_emoji_url(self, emoji):
        if emoji not in self.EMOJI_CODES:
            return None
        code = self.EMOJI_CODES[emoji]
        lib_config = self.LIBRARIES[self.library]
        url_template = lib_config['url_template']
        
        if 'name_map' in lib_config:
            if emoji in lib_config['name_map']:
                name = lib_config['name_map'][emoji]
                return url_template.format(name=name, code=code)
            else:
                return f"https://em-content.zobj.net/thumbs/120/twitter/348/{code}.png"
        return None
    
    def _get_emoji_image(self, emoji):
        cache_key = f"{self.library}_{emoji}"
        if cache_key in self._cache:
            return self._cache[cache_key]
        
        url = self._get_emoji_url(emoji)
        if url is None:
            return None
        
        try:
            response = requests.get(url, timeout=5)
            if response.status_code != 200:
                return None
            img = Image.open(BytesIO(response.content))
            self._cache[cache_key] = img
            return img
        except:
            return None
    
    def add_emoji(self, ax, emoji, x, y, zoom=0.1, xycoords='data'):
        img = self._get_emoji_image(emoji)
        if img is None:
            return None
        imagebox = OffsetImage(img, zoom=zoom)
        ab = AnnotationBbox(imagebox, (x, y), frameon=False, 
                           xycoords=xycoords, box_alignment=(0.5, 0.5))
        ax.add_artist(ab)
        return ab

emoji = MultiEmojiHelper('twemoji')
print("✅ EmojiHelper carregado!")

# ============================================================================
# 2. FUNÇÃO DE ANÁLISE COM EMOJIS COLORIDOS
# ============================================================================

def analyze_emojis_colorful(df, emoji_helper):
    """Análise completa de uso de emojis COM VISUALIZAÇÃO COLORIDA."""
    import emoji as emoji_lib
    from collections import Counter
    
    print("="*80)
    print("😊 ANÁLISE DETALHADA DE EMOJIS")
    print("="*80)
    
    df_text = df[df['conteudo'].notna()].copy()
    
    def extract_emojis(text):
        if pd.isna(text):
            return []
        return [c for c in text if c in emoji_lib.EMOJI_DATA]
    
    print("\n🔍 Extraindo emojis...")
    
    df_text['emojis_list'] = df_text['conteudo'].apply(extract_emojis)
    df_text['emoji_count'] = df_text['emojis_list'].apply(len)
    
    all_emojis = []
    emojis_p1 = []
    emojis_p2 = []
    
    for idx, row in df_text.iterrows():
        emojis = row['emojis_list']
        all_emojis.extend(emojis)
        
        if row['remetente'] == 'P1':
            emojis_p1.extend(emojis)
        else:
            emojis_p2.extend(emojis)
    
    counter_all = Counter(all_emojis)
    counter_p1 = Counter(emojis_p1)
    counter_p2 = Counter(emojis_p2)
    
    print(f"\n📊 Estatísticas Gerais:")
    print(f"   • Total de emojis: {len(all_emojis):,}")
    print(f"   • Emojis únicos: {len(counter_all)}")
    print(f"   • Msgs com emoji: {(df_text['emoji_count'] > 0).sum():,} ({(df_text['emoji_count'] > 0).sum()/len(df_text)*100:.1f}%)")
    print(f"   • Média por mensagem: {df_text['emoji_count'].mean():.2f}")
    
    print(f"\n👥 Por Participante:")
    print(f"   • P1: {len(emojis_p1):,} emojis ({len(emojis_p1)/len(all_emojis)*100:.1f}%)")
    print(f"   • P2: {len(emojis_p2):,} emojis ({len(emojis_p2)/len(all_emojis)*100:.1f}%)")
    
    # ========================================================================
    # VISUALIZAÇÕES
    # ========================================================================
    
    fig = plt.figure(figsize=(20, 14))
    gs = fig.add_gridspec(3, 2, hspace=0.35, wspace=0.3)
    
    # Top 20 Geral
    ax1 = fig.add_subplot(gs[0, :])
    top_20 = counter_all.most_common(20)
    counts_top = [e[1] for e in top_20]
    
    bars = ax1.barh(range(len(top_20)), counts_top, color='#4dabf7', alpha=0.6, edgecolor='black')
    ax1.set_yticks(range(len(top_20)))
    ax1.set_yticklabels([f"     {c:,}" for e, c in top_20], fontsize=12)
    
    for i, (emoji_char, count) in enumerate(top_20):
        emoji_helper.add_emoji(ax1, emoji_char, -max(counts_top)*0.08, i, zoom=0.08)
    
    ax1.set_xlabel('Frequencia', fontsize=12, fontweight='bold')
    ax1.set_title('Top 20 Emojis Mais Usados (Geral)', fontsize=14, fontweight='bold')
    ax1.invert_yaxis()
    ax1.grid(axis='x', alpha=0.3)
    ax1.set_xlim(-max(counts_top)*0.1, max(counts_top)*1.1)
    
    # Top 15 P1
    ax2 = fig.add_subplot(gs[1, 0])
    top_p1 = counter_p1.most_common(15)
    counts_p1_top = [e[1] for e in top_p1]
    
    bars_p1 = ax2.barh(range(len(top_p1)), counts_p1_top, color='#0A0AFF', alpha=0.6, edgecolor='black')
    ax2.set_yticks(range(len(top_p1)))
    ax2.set_yticklabels([f"     {c:,}" for e, c in top_p1], fontsize=11)
    
    for i, (emoji_char, count) in enumerate(top_p1):
        emoji_helper.add_emoji(ax2, emoji_char, -max(counts_p1_top)*0.08, i, zoom=0.08)
    
    ax2.set_xlabel('Frequencia', fontsize=11, fontweight='bold')
    ax2.set_title('Top 15 Emojis P1 (Marlon)', fontsize=13, fontweight='bold', color='#0A0AFF')
    ax2.invert_yaxis()
    ax2.grid(axis='x', alpha=0.3)
    ax2.set_xlim(-max(counts_p1_top)*0.1, max(counts_p1_top)*1.1)
    
    # Top 15 P2
    ax3 = fig.add_subplot(gs[1, 1])
    top_p2 = counter_p2.most_common(15)
    counts_p2_top = [e[1] for e in top_p2]
    
    bars_p2 = ax3.barh(range(len(top_p2)), counts_p2_top, color='#FF0080', alpha=0.6, edgecolor='black')
    ax3.set_yticks(range(len(top_p2)))
    ax3.set_yticklabels([f"     {c:,}" for e, c in top_p2], fontsize=11)
    
    for i, (emoji_char, count) in enumerate(top_p2):
        emoji_helper.add_emoji(ax3, emoji_char, -max(counts_p2_top)*0.08, i, zoom=0.08)
    
    ax3.set_xlabel('Frequencia', fontsize=11, fontweight='bold')
    ax3.set_title('Top 15 Emojis P2 (Leticia)', fontsize=13, fontweight='bold', color='#FF0080')
    ax3.invert_yaxis()
    ax3.grid(axis='x', alpha=0.3)
    ax3.set_xlim(-max(counts_p2_top)*0.1, max(counts_p2_top)*1.1)
    
    # Evolução Mensal
    ax4 = fig.add_subplot(gs[2, :])
    
    df_text['ano_mes'] = df_text['timestamp'].dt.to_period('M')
    emoji_por_mes = df_text.groupby('ano_mes')['emoji_count'].sum()
    msgs_por_mes = df_text.groupby('ano_mes').size()
    emoji_ratio_mes = (emoji_por_mes / msgs_por_mes)
    
    meses_dates = [m.to_timestamp() for m in emoji_por_mes.index]
    
    ax4_twin = ax4.twinx()
    
    line1 = ax4.plot(meses_dates, emoji_por_mes.values, marker='o', linewidth=3, 
                     markersize=8, color='#FF0080', label='Total de Emojis', zorder=3)
    ax4.fill_between(meses_dates, emoji_por_mes.values, alpha=0.2, color='#FF0080')
    
    line2 = ax4_twin.plot(meses_dates, emoji_ratio_mes.values, marker='s', linewidth=2, 
                          markersize=7, color='#0A0AFF', label='Emojis por Mensagem', 
                          linestyle='--', zorder=3)
    
    ax4.set_xlabel('Mes', fontsize=12, fontweight='bold')
    ax4.set_ylabel('Total de Emojis', fontsize=12, fontweight='bold', color='#FF0080')
    ax4_twin.set_ylabel('Emojis por Mensagem', fontsize=12, fontweight='bold', color='#0A0AFF')
    ax4.set_title('Evolucao do Uso de Emojis ao Longo do Ano', fontsize=14, fontweight='bold')
    ax4.grid(True, alpha=0.3)
    
    import matplotlib.dates as mdates
    ax4.xaxis.set_major_formatter(mdates.DateFormatter('%b/%y'))
    ax4.xaxis.set_major_locator(mdates.MonthLocator())
    ax4.tick_params(axis='x', rotation=45)
    
    lines = line1 + line2
    labels = [l.get_label() for l in lines]
    ax4.legend(lines, labels, loc='upper left', fontsize=11)
    
    plt.suptitle('Analise Completa de Emojis\n"A Linguagem Visual da Conexao"', 
                fontsize=16, fontweight='bold', y=0.995)
    
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.show()
    
    df_text.drop(columns=['ano_mes'], inplace=True, errors='ignore')
    
    print(f"\n💎 Emojis Únicos:")
    p1_only = set(counter_p1.keys()) - set(counter_p2.keys())
    p2_only = set(counter_p2.keys()) - set(counter_p1.keys())
    
    print(f"   • Só P1 usa: {len(p1_only)} emojis")
    if len(p1_only) > 0:
        print(f"      {' '.join(list(p1_only)[:20])}")
    
    print(f"   • Só P2 usa: {len(p2_only)} emojis")
    if len(p2_only) > 0:
        print(f"      {' '.join(list(p2_only)[:20])}")
    
    print("\n" + "="*80)
    
    return counter_all, counter_p1, counter_p2

# ============================================================================
# 3. EXECUTA
# ============================================================================

emoji_all, emoji_p1, emoji_p2 = analyze_emojis_colorful(df, emoji)
```

-------------------------------------------------------
-------------------------------------------------------


# Teste de fontes

```{python}
#| label: teste-emojis-fontes
#| fig-cap: "Teste de Fontes com Emojis"
#| fig-height: 8

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from matplotlib import font_manager
import sys

# Lista de fontes para testar
fontes_testar = [
    'DejaVu Sans',           # Padrão matplotlib
    'Segoe UI Emoji',        # Windows
    'Apple Color Emoji',     # Mac
    'Noto Color Emoji',      # Linux/Android
    'Symbola',               # Linux alternativa
    'Arial Unicode MS',      # Multiplataforma
]

# Texto com emojis para testar
texto_teste = "📊 Gráfico 🎨 Arte ❤️ Amor 🚀 Foguete ⭐ Estrela 📅 Data"

# Cria figura
fig, axes = plt.subplots(len(fontes_testar) + 2, 1, figsize=(12, 10))
fig.suptitle('TESTE DE EMOJIS EM DIFERENTES FONTES', fontsize=16, fontweight='bold')

# Testa cada fonte
for i, fonte in enumerate(fontes_testar):
    ax = axes[i]
    ax.axis('off')
    
    # Verifica se fonte existe no sistema
    fontes_disponiveis = [f.name for f in font_manager.fontManager.ttflist]
    existe = fonte in fontes_disponiveis
    
    try:
        plt.rcParams['font.family'] = fonte
        
        if existe:
            status = "✓ DISPONÍVEL"
            cor = 'green'
        else:
            status = "✗ NÃO ENCONTRADA"
            cor = 'red'
        
        ax.text(0.05, 0.5, f"{fonte}: {texto_teste}", 
               fontsize=12, va='center',
               bbox=dict(boxstyle='round,pad=0.5', 
                        facecolor='white', 
                        edgecolor=cor, 
                        linewidth=2))
        
        ax.text(0.95, 0.5, status, 
               fontsize=10, va='center', ha='right',
               color=cor, fontweight='bold')
               
    except Exception as e:
        ax.text(0.05, 0.5, f"{fonte}: ERRO - {str(e)[:50]}", 
               fontsize=10, va='center', color='red')

# Teste com símbolos Unicode simples (sem emoji)
ax = axes[-2]
ax.axis('off')
plt.rcParams['font.family'] = 'DejaVu Sans'
simbolos_unicode = "★ ● ◆ ▶ ■ → ✓ ← ↑ ↓"
ax.text(0.05, 0.5, f"Unicode Simples (sempre funciona): {simbolos_unicode}", 
       fontsize=12, va='center',
       bbox=dict(boxstyle='round,pad=0.5', 
                facecolor='lightgreen', 
                edgecolor='darkgreen', 
                linewidth=2))

# Teste com LaTeX
ax = axes[-1]
ax.axis('off')
simbolos_latex = r"$\bigstar$ $\bullet$ $\clubsuit$ $\heartsuit$ $\spadesuit$ $\blacktriangleright$"
ax.text(0.05, 0.5, f"Símbolos LaTeX (sempre funciona): {simbolos_latex}", 
       fontsize=12, va='center',
       bbox=dict(boxstyle='round,pad=0.5', 
                facecolor='lightblue', 
                edgecolor='darkblue', 
                linewidth=2))

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

# Restaura fonte padrão
plt.rcParams['font.family'] = 'DejaVu Sans'

# ========================================================================
# LISTA TODAS AS FONTES DISPONÍVEIS NO SISTEMA
# ========================================================================

print("\n" + "="*70)
print("📋 FONTES DISPONÍVEIS NO SISTEMA:")
print("="*70)

fontes_sistema = sorted(set([f.name for f in font_manager.fontManager.ttflist]))

# Filtra fontes que podem ter emoji
fontes_emoji = [f for f in fontes_sistema if any(palavra in f.lower() 
                for palavra in ['emoji', 'color', 'symbol', 'unicode', 'noto'])]

if fontes_emoji:
    print("\n🎨 Fontes com possível suporte a emoji:")
    for fonte in fontes_emoji:
        print(f"   • {fonte}")
else:
    print("\n❌ Nenhuma fonte com emoji detectada no sistema")

print(f"\n📊 Total de fontes no sistema: {len(fontes_sistema)}")
print(f"💻 Sistema operacional: {sys.platform}")

# Mostra algumas fontes comuns
print("\n📝 Fontes comuns disponíveis:")
fontes_comuns = ['Arial', 'Times New Roman', 'Courier New', 'Verdana', 
                 'DejaVu Sans', 'Liberation Sans']
for fonte in fontes_comuns:
    if fonte in fontes_sistema:
        print(f"   ✓ {fonte}")
    else:
        print(f"   ✗ {fonte}")

print("="*70)
```

```{python}

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.font_manager as fm
import platform

def find_emoji_font():
    """Dynamically finds the best available emoji font for the current OS."""
    system = platform.system()
    if system == 'Windows':
        font_name = 'Segoe UI Emoji'
    elif system == 'Darwin':  # macOS
        font_name = 'Apple Color Emoji'
    else:  # Linux or others
        # Generic approach for systems that might use Noto Color Emoji
        font_name = 'Noto Color Emoji'

    # Check if the desired font is actually available on the system
    font_path = fm.findfont(fm.FontProperties(family=font_name, weight='regular'))
    if font_path:
        return font_name
    else:
        # Fallback to a generic sans-serif family and hope the OS handles substitution
        print(f"Warning: '{font_name}' not found. Falling back to default sans-serif.")
        return 'sans-serif'

# Use the function to set the font parameter
emoji_font = find_emoji_font()
plt.rcParams['font.family'] = emoji_font

# Example plot usage:
fig, ax = plt.subplots()
ax.text(0.5, 0.5, 'Testando Emojis: ⚠️✅🚀', fontsize=30, ha='center', va='center')
ax.set_title(f'Rendering with font: {plt.rcParams["font.family"]}')
# plt.show() # Uncomment to display the plot in a GUI environment

```


```{python}
#| label: teste-arial-unicode
#| fig-cap: "Teste Arial Unicode MS"

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 4))
ax.axis('off')

# Testa Arial Unicode MS
plt.rcParams['font.family'] = 'Arial Unicode MS'

texto_teste = "📊 Gráfico 🎨 Arte ❤️ Amor 🚀 Foguete ⭐ Estrela"

ax.text(0.5, 0.7, "Teste com Arial Unicode MS:", 
       fontsize=14, ha='center', fontweight='bold')

ax.text(0.5, 0.5, texto_teste, 
       fontsize=16, ha='center',
       bbox=dict(boxstyle='round,pad=0.8', 
                facecolor='lightyellow', 
                edgecolor='orange', 
                linewidth=2))

ax.text(0.5, 0.3, "Se você ver caixinhas □ em vez de emojis,\nemojis NÃO funcionam no matplotlib", 
       fontsize=11, ha='center', style='italic', color='gray')

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

# Restaura fonte
plt.rcParams['font.family'] = 'DejaVu Sans'
```


```{python}
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from matplotlib import font_manager

# Força matplotlib a recarregar fontes
font_manager._load_fontmanager(try_read_cache=False)

# Testa Noto Color Emoji
plt.rcParams['font.family'] = 'Noto Color Emoji'

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 3))
ax.axis('off')

texto = "📊 Gráfico 🎨 Arte ❤️ Amor 🚀 Foguete ⭐ Estrela 📅 Data"
ax.text(0.5, 0.5, texto, fontsize=20, ha='center')

plt.show()
```



```{python}
#| label: rebuild-font-cache

import matplotlib
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from matplotlib import font_manager
import os

# Remove cache antigo
cache_dir = matplotlib.get_cachedir()
print(f"Cache dir: {cache_dir}")

# Lista arquivos de cache
import glob
cache_files = glob.glob(os.path.join(cache_dir, 'font*.json*'))
print(f"\nArquivos de cache encontrados: {len(cache_files)}")

for f in cache_files:
    try:
        os.remove(f)
        print(f"✓ Removido: {os.path.basename(f)}")
    except:
        print(f"✗ Erro ao remover: {os.path.basename(f)}")

# Força reconstrução
print("\n🔄 Reconstruindo cache de fontes...")
font_manager._load_fontmanager(try_read_cache=False)

# Lista fontes com 'emoji' no nome
fontes_emoji = [f.name for f in font_manager.fontManager.ttflist 
                if 'emoji' in f.name.lower() or 'color' in f.name.lower()]

print(f"\n📝 Fontes com emoji encontradas: {len(fontes_emoji)}")
for fonte in fontes_emoji:
    print(f"   • {fonte}")

# Procura também 'Noto' e 'Apple'
fontes_noto = [f.name for f in font_manager.fontManager.ttflist 
               if 'noto' in f.name.lower()]
print(f"\n📝 Fontes Noto encontradas: {len(fontes_noto)}")
for fonte in fontes_noto[:5]:  # Mostra só as 5 primeiras
    print(f"   • {fonte}")

print("\n✅ Reconstrução completa!")
print("⚠️  REINICIE o kernel do Python agora!")
```



```{python}
#| label: teste-emoji-final

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from matplotlib import font_manager

# Tenta diferentes variações do nome
fontes_tentar = [
    'Noto Color Emoji',
    'NotoColorEmoji', 
    'Noto Emoji',
    'Apple Color Emoji',
    'AppleColorEmoji',
]

fig, axes = plt.subplots(len(fontes_tentar), 1, figsize=(12, 8))
texto_emoji = "📊 📈 📉 💰 ❤️ 🎨 🚀 ⭐ 📅 ✅"

for i, fonte in enumerate(fontes_tentar):
    ax = axes[i]
    ax.axis('off')
    
    try:
        plt.rcParams['font.family'] = fonte
        ax.text(0.05, 0.5, f"{fonte}: {texto_emoji}", 
               fontsize=16, va='center')
    except Exception as e:
        ax.text(0.05, 0.5, f"{fonte}: ERRO", 
               fontsize=12, va='center', color='red')

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

# Restaura
plt.rcParams['font.family'] = 'DejaVu Sans'
```

```{python}
#| label: emoji-como-imagem

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from matplotlib.offsetbox import OffsetImage, AnnotationBbox
from PIL import Image
import requests
from io import BytesIO

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 6))
ax.set_xlim(0, 10)
ax.set_ylim(0, 10)

# URLs de emojis (Twemoji - open source da Twitter)
emojis_urls = {
    'grafico': 'https://em-content.zobj.net/thumbs/120/twitter/348/bar-chart_1f4ca.png',
    'coracao': 'https://em-content.zobj.net/thumbs/120/twitter/348/red-heart_2764-fe0f.png',
    'foguete': 'https://em-content.zobj.net/thumbs/120/twitter/348/rocket_1f680.png',
    'estrela': 'https://em-content.zobj.net/thumbs/120/twitter/348/star_2b50.png',
    'calendario': 'https://em-content.zobj.net/thumbs/120/twitter/348/calendar_1f4c5.png',
}

# Adiciona cada emoji
posicoes = [(2, 8), (4, 8), (6, 8), (8, 8), (5, 5)]
labels = ['Gráfico', 'Amor', 'Foguete', 'Estrela', 'Data']

for (x, y), (nome, url), label in zip(posicoes, emojis_urls.items(), labels):
    # Baixa emoji
    response = requests.get(url)
    img = Image.open(BytesIO(response.content))
    
    # Adiciona ao gráfico
    imagebox = OffsetImage(img, zoom=0.3)
    ab = AnnotationBbox(imagebox, (x, y), frameon=False)
    ax.add_artist(ab)
    
    # Adiciona label
    ax.text(x, y-1, label, ha='center', fontsize=12, fontweight='bold')

ax.set_title('Emojis como IMAGENS (sempre funciona!)', 
             fontsize=16, fontweight='bold', pad=20)
ax.axis('off')

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```




```{python}
from imojify import imojify
from matplotlib import pyplot as plt
from matplotlib.offsetbox import OffsetImage, AnnotationBbox

def add_emoji(ax, emoji, x, y, zoom=0.1):
    img_path = imojify.get_img_path(emoji)
    img = plt.imread(img_path)
    im = OffsetImage(img, zoom=zoom)
    im.image.axes = ax
    ab = AnnotationBbox(im, (x, y), frameon=False, pad=0)
    ax.add_artist(ab)

# Uso:
fig, ax = plt.subplots()
add_emoji(ax, '🥰', 0.5, 0.5)
```





## 2️⃣ Análise Detalhada de Emojis COM EMOJIS COLORIDOS! 🎨
```{python}
#| label: analise-emojis-coloridos
#| fig-cap: "Análise Completa de Emojis (com emojis coloridos!)"
#| fig-height: 14

def analyze_emojis_colorful(df, emoji_helper):
    """
    Análise completa de uso de emojis COM VISUALIZAÇÃO COLORIDA.
    """
    import re
    import emoji as emoji_lib  # Renomeado para não conflitar
    from collections import Counter
    
    print("="*80)
    print("😊 ANÁLISE DETALHADA DE EMOJIS")
    print("="*80)
    
    # Filtra mensagens com conteúdo
    df_text = df[df['conteudo'].notna()].copy()
    
    # ========================================================================
    # EXTRAI TODOS OS EMOJIS
    # ========================================================================
    
    def extract_emojis(text):
        """Extrai emojis de um texto."""
        if pd.isna(text):
            return []
        return [c for c in text if c in emoji_lib.EMOJI_DATA]
    
    print("\n🔍 Extraindo emojis...")
    
    df_text['emojis_list'] = df_text['conteudo'].apply(extract_emojis)
    df_text['emoji_count'] = df_text['emojis_list'].apply(len)
    
    # Todos os emojis
    all_emojis = []
    emojis_p1 = []
    emojis_p2 = []
    
    for idx, row in df_text.iterrows():
        emojis = row['emojis_list']
        all_emojis.extend(emojis)
        
        if row['remetente'] == 'P1':
            emojis_p1.extend(emojis)
        else:
            emojis_p2.extend(emojis)
    
    # Contadores
    counter_all = Counter(all_emojis)
    counter_p1 = Counter(emojis_p1)
    counter_p2 = Counter(emojis_p2)
    
    print(f"\n📊 Estatísticas Gerais:")
    print(f"   • Total de emojis: {len(all_emojis):,}")
    print(f"   • Emojis únicos: {len(counter_all)}")
    print(f"   • Msgs com emoji: {(df_text['emoji_count'] > 0).sum():,} ({(df_text['emoji_count'] > 0).sum()/len(df_text)*100:.1f}%)")
    print(f"   • Média por mensagem: {df_text['emoji_count'].mean():.2f}")
    
    print(f"\n👥 Por Participante:")
    print(f"   • P1: {len(emojis_p1):,} emojis ({len(emojis_p1)/len(all_emojis)*100:.1f}%)")
    print(f"   • P2: {len(emojis_p2):,} emojis ({len(emojis_p2)/len(all_emojis)*100:.1f}%)")
    
    # ========================================================================
    # VISUALIZAÇÕES COM EMOJIS COLORIDOS!
    # ========================================================================
    
    fig = plt.figure(figsize=(20, 14))
    gs = fig.add_gridspec(3, 2, hspace=0.35, wspace=0.3)
    
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------------
    # 1. Top 20 Emojis GERAL (COM EMOJIS COLORIDOS!)
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------------
    ax1 = fig.add_subplot(gs[0, :])
    
    top_20 = counter_all.most_common(20)
    emojis_top = [e[0] for e in top_20]
    counts_top = [e[1] for e in top_20]
    
    bars = ax1.barh(range(len(top_20)), counts_top, color='#4dabf7', alpha=0.6, edgecolor='black')
    
    # ADICIONA EMOJIS COLORIDOS NO EIXO Y!
    ax1.set_yticks(range(len(top_20)))
    ax1.set_yticklabels([f"     {c:,}" for e, c in top_20], fontsize=12)  # Só número
    
    # Adiciona emojis como IMAGENS
    for i, (emoji_char, count) in enumerate(top_20):
        emoji_helper.add_emoji(ax1, emoji_char, -max(counts_top)*0.08, i, zoom=0.08)
    
    ax1.set_xlabel('Frequência', fontsize=12, fontweight='bold')
    ax1.set_title('Top 20 Emojis Mais Usados (Geral)', fontsize=14, fontweight='bold')
    ax1.invert_yaxis()
    ax1.grid(axis='x', alpha=0.3)
    ax1.set_xlim(-max(counts_top)*0.1, max(counts_top)*1.1)
    
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------------
    # 2. Top 15 P1 (COM EMOJIS COLORIDOS!)
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------------
    ax2 = fig.add_subplot(gs[1, 0])
    
    top_p1 = counter_p1.most_common(15)
    counts_p1_top = [e[1] for e in top_p1]
    
    bars_p1 = ax2.barh(range(len(top_p1)), counts_p1_top, color='#0A0AFF', alpha=0.6, edgecolor='black')
    
    ax2.set_yticks(range(len(top_p1)))
    ax2.set_yticklabels([f"     {c:,}" for e, c in top_p1], fontsize=11)
    
    # Emojis coloridos
    for i, (emoji_char, count) in enumerate(top_p1):
        emoji_helper.add_emoji(ax2, emoji_char, -max(counts_p1_top)*0.08, i, zoom=0.08)
    
    ax2.set_xlabel('Frequência', fontsize=11, fontweight='bold')
    ax2.set_title('Top 15 Emojis P1 (Marlon)', fontsize=13, fontweight='bold', color='#0A0AFF')
    ax2.invert_yaxis()
    ax2.grid(axis='x', alpha=0.3)
    ax2.set_xlim(-max(counts_p1_top)*0.1, max(counts_p1_top)*1.1)
    
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------------
    # 3. Top 15 P2 (COM EMOJIS COLORIDOS!)
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------------
    ax3 = fig.add_subplot(gs[1, 1])
    
    top_p2 = counter_p2.most_common(15)
    counts_p2_top = [e[1] for e in top_p2]
    
    bars_p2 = ax3.barh(range(len(top_p2)), counts_p2_top, color='#FF0080', alpha=0.6, edgecolor='black')
    
    ax3.set_yticks(range(len(top_p2)))
    ax3.set_yticklabels([f"     {c:,}" for e, c in top_p2], fontsize=11)
    
    # Emojis coloridos
    for i, (emoji_char, count) in enumerate(top_p2):
        emoji_helper.add_emoji(ax3, emoji_char, -max(counts_p2_top)*0.08, i, zoom=0.08)
    
    ax3.set_xlabel('Frequência', fontsize=11, fontweight='bold')
    ax3.set_title('Top 15 Emojis P2 (Letícia)', fontsize=13, fontweight='bold', color='#FF0080')
    ax3.invert_yaxis()
    ax3.grid(axis='x', alpha=0.3)
    ax3.set_xlim(-max(counts_p2_top)*0.1, max(counts_p2_top)*1.1)
    
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------------
    # 4. Evolução Mensal (emoji no título!)
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------------
    ax4 = fig.add_subplot(gs[2, :])
    
    df_text['ano_mes'] = df_text['timestamp'].dt.to_period('M')
    emoji_por_mes = df_text.groupby('ano_mes')['emoji_count'].sum()
    msgs_por_mes = df_text.groupby('ano_mes').size()
    emoji_ratio_mes = (emoji_por_mes / msgs_por_mes)
    
    meses_dates = [m.to_timestamp() for m in emoji_por_mes.index]
    
    ax4_twin = ax4.twinx()
    
    # Linha: total de emojis
    line1 = ax4.plot(meses_dates, emoji_por_mes.values, marker='o', linewidth=3, 
                     markersize=8, color='#FF0080', label='Total de Emojis', zorder=3)
    ax4.fill_between(meses_dates, emoji_por_mes.values, alpha=0.2, color='#FF0080')
    
    # Linha: ratio
    line2 = ax4_twin.plot(meses_dates, emoji_ratio_mes.values, marker='s', linewidth=2, 
                          markersize=7, color='#0A0AFF', label='Emojis por Mensagem', 
                          linestyle='--', zorder=3)
    
    ax4.set_xlabel('Mes', fontsize=12, fontweight='bold')
    ax4.set_ylabel('Total de Emojis', fontsize=12, fontweight='bold', color='#FF0080')
    ax4_twin.set_ylabel('Emojis por Mensagem', fontsize=12, fontweight='bold', color='#0A0AFF')
    ax4.set_title('Evolucao do Uso de Emojis ao Longo do Ano', fontsize=14, fontweight='bold')
    ax4.grid(True, alpha=0.3)
    
    # Formata eixo X
    import matplotlib.dates as mdates
    ax4.xaxis.set_major_formatter(mdates.DateFormatter('%b/%y'))
    ax4.xaxis.set_major_locator(mdates.MonthLocator())
    ax4.tick_params(axis='x', rotation=45)
    
    # Legenda combinada
    lines = line1 + line2
    labels = [l.get_label() for l in lines]
    ax4.legend(lines, labels, loc='upper left', fontsize=11)
    
    plt.suptitle('Analise Completa de Emojis\n"A Linguagem Visual da Conexao"', 
                fontsize=16, fontweight='bold', y=0.995)
    
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.show()
    
    # Limpa coluna temporária
    df_text.drop(columns=['ano_mes'], inplace=True, errors='ignore')
    
    # Emojis únicos
    print(f"\n💎 Emojis Únicos:")
    p1_only = set(counter_p1.keys()) - set(counter_p2.keys())
    p2_only = set(counter_p2.keys()) - set(counter_p1.keys())
    
    print(f"   • Só P1 usa: {len(p1_only)} emojis")
    if len(p1_only) > 0:
        print(f"      {' '.join(list(p1_only)[:20])}")
    
    print(f"   • Só P2 usa: {len(p2_only)} emojis")
    if len(p2_only) > 0:
        print(f"      {' '.join(list(p2_only)[:20])}")
    
    print("\n" + "="*80)
    
    return counter_all, counter_p1, counter_p2

# Executa análise COM EMOJIS COLORIDOS!
emoji_all, emoji_p1, emoji_p2 = analyze_emojis_colorful(df, emoji)
```
